notes: ISO publishes globally recognized information security standards. The ISO/IEC 27000 series is the most widely adopted information security management framework. ISO standards are not freely available — must be purchased from ISO or national standards bodies.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27001 Information Security Management
data.description: The global certification standard for information security management systems (ISMS). Annex A contains normative control objectives.
data.notes: ISO 27001 is a certification standard — organizations can be audited and certified. Version 2022 restructured from 14 domains to 4 themes: Organizational (A.5, 37 controls), People (A.6, 8 controls), Physical (A.7, 14 controls), Technological (A.8, 34 controls). Maps to NIST CSF, CIS Controls, and other frameworks. Many contracts and regulations require ISO 27001 certification.
data.version_disambiguation: The 2022 version restructured significantly: 4 themes with 93 controls (was 14 domains with 114 controls in 2013). Control numbering changed between versions — always specify version.

versions_available.note: 4 themes (Organizational, People, Physical, Technological), 93 Annex A controls. Major restructuring from 2013.
14 domains (A.5 through A.18), 114 controls. Still referenced in older compliance documentation and certifications.

children.description: ISO 27001 Annex A theme/domain prefix (e.g., A.5, A.8). Groups related controls.

data.note: No per-theme URL — ISO standards must be purchased. In 2022: A.5=Organizational, A.6=People, A.7=Physical, A.8=Technological. In 2013: A.5 through A.18 represented 14 domains.

children.description: ISO 27001 Annex A control ID. 2022 uses A.N.N (two-level), 2013 uses A.N.N.N (three-level).

data.note: No per-control URL — ISO standards must be purchased. Some derivative resources and summaries are freely available.

examples.note: Policies for information security (2013 numbering — deeper hierarchy)

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27002 Information Security Controls
data.description: Implementation guidance for ISO 27001 Annex A controls. Not a certification standard.
data.notes: Control numbering matches Annex A minus the 'A.' prefix. 27001 says what to do, 27002 says how to do it. For example: 27001 A.5.1 maps to 27002 5.1.

versions_available.note: Aligned with 27001:2022. 4 themes, 93 controls. Major restructuring from 2013.

children.description: ISO 27002 control ID (Theme.Control). Numbering matches 27001 Annex A without the 'A.' prefix.

data.note: No per-control URL — ISO standards must be purchased. Numbering matches 27001 Annex A without the 'A.' prefix.

examples.note: Policies for information security — implementation guidance for 27001 A.5.1
Privileged access rights — implementation guidance for 27001 A.8.2

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27017 Code of practice for cloud services information security

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27017 Information technology — Security techniques — Code of practice for information security controls based on ISO/IEC 27002 for cloud services
data.description: Cloud-specific implementation guidance for ISO/IEC 27002 controls, plus additional cloud-only controls. Addresses both cloud service customers and cloud service providers.
data.notes: Extends 27002:2013 with cloud-specific guidance. Two ID forms: (1) numeric IDs that reference 27002:2013 clause numbers with cloud implementation guidance, and (2) 'CLD.' prefixed IDs in Annex A for controls additional to 27002. Aligned to 27002:2013 numbering — has not been re-aligned to 27002:2022's restructured numbering. ISO standards must be purchased.

versions_available.note: First and only edition. Aligned with ISO/IEC 27002:2013.

children.description: ISO 27017 reference to a 27002:2013 control with cloud-specific implementation guidance. Numbering matches 27002:2013.

data.note: These IDs reference existing 27002:2013 control numbers — 27017 adds cloud-specific implementation guidance for the same control. To resolve the actual control wording, see 27002:2013 with the same ID.

examples.note: Information security roles and responsibilities (cloud-specific guidance)

children.description: ISO 27017 Annex A cloud-extended control (CLD. prefix). These are controls additional to 27002, not extensions of existing 27002 controls.

data.note: CLD-prefixed controls in Annex A are net-new cloud-specific controls, not 27002 references. The 'CLD.' prefix is part of the canonical ID and must be preserved.

examples.note: Shared roles and responsibilities within a cloud computing environment

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27018 Code of practice for protection of PII in public clouds

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27018 Information technology — Security techniques — Code of practice for protection of personally identifiable information (PII) in public clouds acting as PII processors
data.description: PII-protection implementation guidance for ISO/IEC 27002 controls when public cloud service providers act as PII processors. Aligned with GDPR and other privacy frameworks.
data.notes: Extends 27002:2013 with PII-protection guidance for public cloud PII processors. Includes an Annex A 'Public cloud PII processor extended control set' organized around privacy principles. Aligned to 27002:2013 numbering — has not been re-aligned to 27002:2022's restructured numbering. ISO standards must be purchased. The Annex A extended PII control ID structure is not yet encoded here — see the standard directly for those IDs.

children.description: ISO 27018 reference to a 27002:2013 control with PII-processor implementation guidance. Numbering matches 27002:2013.

data.note: These IDs reference existing 27002:2013 control numbers — 27018 adds PII-specific guidance for the same control. To resolve the actual control wording, see 27002:2013 with the same ID.

examples.note: Information security roles and responsibilities (PII processor guidance)

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 42001 AI Management System
data.description: Management system standard for AI. Provides requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining and continually improving an AI management system.
data.notes: Published 2023. First international standard for AI management. Addresses AI-specific risks including bias, transparency, and safety.

versions_available.note: First edition. First international certification standard for AI management systems.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 23894 AI Risk Management
data.description: Guidance on AI risk management processes.

versions_available.note: First edition. Guidance on managing risk specifically related to AI.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 24028 AI Trustworthiness
data.description: Overview of trustworthiness in AI, including bias, transparency, and accountability.

versions_available.note: First edition. Overview of trustworthiness in artificial intelligence.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 24029 AI Robustness
data.description: Assessment of robustness of neural networks.

versions_available.note: First edition. Methods for assessing robustness of neural networks.

data.official_name: ISO/SAE 21434 - Road vehicles - Cybersecurity engineering
data.notes: Paywalled. Joint ISO/SAE standard. Specifies engineering requirements for cybersecurity risk management in road-vehicle E/E systems throughout the vehicle lifecycle. Companion to UNECE R155 (which references it).

data.official_name: ISO 22301 - Security and resilience - Business continuity management systems - Requirements
data.notes: Paywalled. The international BCM standard. Specifies requirements for an organization's business continuity management system (BCMS). Frequently cited alongside ISO/IEC 27031, NFPA 1600, BS 65000, and ASIS SPC.1.

data.official_name: ISO 22317 - Security and resilience - Business continuity management systems - Guidelines for business impact analysis
data.notes: Paywalled. Companion to ISO 22301; methodology for conducting BIA within a BCMS.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 22989 - Information technology - Artificial intelligence - Artificial intelligence concepts and terminology
data.notes: Paywalled. Foundational AI terminology standard; underpins ISO/IEC 23053, 23894, 42001, 5338, and other AI-series documents.

data.official_name: ISO 26262 - Road vehicles - Functional safety
data.notes: Paywalled. Multi-part series. Functional-safety counterpart to ISO/SAE 21434 (cybersecurity engineering). Frequently cited together in automotive system design.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27003 - Information security management systems - Guidance
data.notes: Paywalled. Implementation guidance for ISO/IEC 27001.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27004 - Information security management - Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
data.notes: Paywalled. Measurement and metrics guidance for ISMS effectiveness.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27005 - Guidance on managing information security risks
data.notes: Paywalled. The risk-management companion to ISO/IEC 27001; cited in nearly every ISMS implementation. Aligned with ISO 31000.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27006 — Requirements for ISMS certification bodies

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27006 - Requirements for bodies providing audit and certification of information security management systems
data.notes: Paywalled. The standard that ISMS certification bodies must meet. Multi-part (27006-1 main, 27006-2 for PIMS audit additions).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27011 — Information security controls for telecom

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27011 - Code of practice for Information security controls based on ISO/IEC 27002 for telecommunications organizations
data.notes: Paywalled. Telecom-sector adaptation of ISO/IEC 27002. Technical basis: ITU-T X.1051 (see secid:control/itu.int).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27019 — Information security controls for energy utility industry

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27019 - Information security controls for the energy utility industry
data.notes: Paywalled. Energy-sector adaptation of ISO/IEC 27002, covering process control systems used to operate energy generation, transmission, and distribution.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27031 — ICT readiness for business continuity

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27031 - Guidelines for information and communication technology readiness for business continuity
data.notes: Paywalled. ICT-focused continuity guidance; complements ISO 22301 (BCMS).

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27032 - Cybersecurity - Guidelines for Internet security
data.notes: Paywalled. Broad framing for cybersecurity collaboration across stakeholders; revised in 2023 with sharper scope.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27035 - Information security incident management
data.notes: Paywalled. Multi-part series: 27035-1 (principles), 27035-2 (incident response readiness), 27035-3 (ICT incident response operations), 27035-4 (coordination).

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27036 - Information security for supplier relationships
data.notes: Paywalled. Multi-part: 27036-1 (overview), 27036-2 (requirements), 27036-3 (ICT supply chain security), 27036-4 (cloud services).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27037 — Digital evidence identification and collection

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27037 - Guidelines for identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of digital evidence
data.notes: Paywalled. Foundational digital-forensics guidance; companion to 27041, 27042, 27043.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27039 — Intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDPS)

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27039 - Selection, deployment and operations of intrusion detection and prevention systems
data.notes: Paywalled.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27040 - Storage security
data.notes: Paywalled. Storage-specific security guidance — particularly relevant to cloud storage services and data-at-rest controls.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27041 - Guidance on assuring suitability and adequacy of incident investigative method
data.notes: Paywalled. Part of the digital forensics / IR series (27037, 27041, 27042, 27043).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27042 — Digital evidence analysis and interpretation

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27042 - Guidelines for the analysis and interpretation of digital evidence
data.notes: Paywalled. Companion to 27037 (collection) and 27041 (process assurance).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27043 — Incident investigation principles and processes

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27043 - Incident investigation principles and processes
data.notes: Paywalled. The overarching investigation-process framework in the 27037/27041/27042/27043 cluster.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27050 - Electronic discovery
data.notes: Paywalled. Multi-part series: 27050-1 (overview), 27050-2 (governance), 27050-3 (code of practice), 27050-4 (technical readiness).

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27099 — Information security controls for PKI/trust service providers

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27099 - Public key infrastructure - Practices and policy framework
data.notes: Paywalled. Information-security controls catalogue specifically for PKI/trust-service operations.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27100 - Cybersecurity - Overview and concepts
data.notes: Paywalled. Higher-level cybersecurity framing and concept dictionary; positioned above 27032.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27102 - Information security management - Guidelines for cyber insurance
data.notes: Paywalled. Bridge between information-security risk and cyber-insurance underwriting/claims practice.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27103 - Cybersecurity and ISO and IEC Standards
data.notes: Paywalled. Cross-mapping of ISO/IEC security standards to the NIST Cybersecurity Framework structure.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27110 - Cybersecurity framework development guidelines
data.notes: Paywalled. Meta-standard: guidance for organizations developing their own cybersecurity framework (e.g., the NIST CSF).

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27400 - Cybersecurity - IoT security and privacy - Guidelines
data.notes: Paywalled. Security and privacy guidance for IoT systems and platforms.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27402 — IoT baseline requirements for consumer devices

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 27402 - Cybersecurity - IoT security and privacy - Device baseline requirements
data.notes: Paywalled. Baseline cybersecurity requirements for IoT consumer devices.

data.official_name: ISO 28000 - Security and resilience - Security management systems - Requirements
data.notes: Paywalled. Supply-chain security management system specification; updated 2022 to broaden from logistics to general security management.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 30141 - Internet of Things (IoT) - Reference architecture
data.notes: Paywalled. Reference architecture for IoT systems — entities, domains, characteristics.

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 38500 - Information technology - Governance of IT for the organization
data.notes: Paywalled. The international IT-governance standard. COBIT is the most-cited implementation framework that satisfies 38500's principles.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 38507 — Governance implications of AI for organizations

data.official_name: ISO/IEC 38507 - Governance implications of the use of artificial intelligence by organizations
data.notes: Paywalled. AI-governance counterpart to ISO/IEC 38500; companion to ISO/IEC 42001.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC TS 5469 — AI functional safety and AI systems

data.official_name: ISO/IEC TS 5469 - Artificial intelligence - Functional safety and AI systems
data.notes: Paywalled. Technical Specification on safety considerations when AI is used in safety-related systems. Emerging area at the intersection of ISO 26262 (functional safety) and the ISO/IEC 22989/42001 AI series.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC TR 24368 — Overview of ethical and societal concerns in AI

data.official_name: ISO/IEC TR 24368 - Information technology - Artificial intelligence - Overview of ethical and societal concerns
data.notes: Paywalled Technical Report. Catalogue of ethical and societal concerns related to AI; intended as background reading for AI risk and impact assessments.
